title: Decision Record
description: An immutable record of one governed decision. The payload is never mutated: corrections create a superseding record, and lifecycle state is derived from the append-only transition log rather than stored here. The outcome/strategy relation is enforced structurally by the allOf clauses below, not merely by convention.

amendment_ref.description: Amendment governing this decision, when one applies.

strategy.description: Present if and only if outcome is GROUNDED. REFUSED carries no strategy: absence is the correct representation, and a third 'refusal' strategy would duplicate the outcome across two semantic axes.

reason_code.description: Controlled reason code from the governing contract's reason-code set. Required when outcome is REFUSED.

verification_mechanism.description: Name of the mechanism that produced the claim verdicts, so a decision can be attributed to the verifier that made it.

claim_counts.description: Counts by verdict. Null total means verification never ran; zero means it ran and produced no claims. The distinction is load-bearing and is why these are nullable rather than defaulted to zero.

latency_ms.description: Wall time of the verification call only. Not retrieval, rendering, or persistence.

supersedes.description: Set on a successor record. Supersession is recorded by the successor and by an append-only transition; the predecessor is never edited.

allOf.$comment: ET-OUT-001 / ET-OUT-003: GROUNDED requires a strategy; REFUSED forbids one and requires a reason code.
ET-OUT-002: REFUSED must carry no strategy and must carry a controlled reason code.
